title: Net-New Story Gap Review — 2026-05-15 (HRREC-82977)
content:

HRREC-92002

31% — limited tension in this heuristic pass; lenses largely agree
  • Jira — Review BDD Scenarios vs compose/send/notification siblings for overlapping REST contract.

  • Salomon — (Replay placeholder—not live Salomon excerpts.) In a live run, search Salomon on this key’s words—not a generic inbox checklist.

For HRREC-92002, relate the catalog hits above to the REST/UI slice in Summary—treat hits as direction, not shipped proof. Risk — Same catalog-level risk as rows above—confirm wiring for this key.
  • Finding: No strong cross-lens conflict showed up in this heuristic replay.
  • Recommended next step: Re-read Jira for “Visual rendering of the basic message t...” on HRREC-92002; if nothing new surfaces, keep scenarios as written.

Beyond Jira — Visual rendering of the basic message thread (UI Only)
Given A recruiter with Modify/Send on conversational email where this slice applies on a tenant staged for this epic; Jira’s first *Given* already includes: the recruiter has opened the sliding side pane...
When I click Send on the recruiting email draft for the states described in Jira (attachments only if this slice owns them)
Then Observable UI, notification, or persisted thread state matches the acceptance criteria for this slice in recruiter-verifiable terms (harden wording under 430 / 319 before sign-off)

HRREC-92003
PH: Recruiter sees error messages if their email has bounced

36% — minor residual signals—confirm in a live evidence pass
  • Jira — bounce/complaint telemetry — confirm surfaced strings vs silent drop.; attachments — align client vs server size/type rejection.
  • Salomon — (Replay placeholder—not live Salomon excerpts.) If support-style threads exist, confirm how this slice surfaces delivery failure in plain recruiter language.
  • Deployment Agent — (Replay placeholder—not live Deployment Agent answers.) In a live review, ask DA which admin toggles mirror common customer misconfiguration for this area.
  • Jira — Add one negative path tied to this summary.
  • Salomon — (Replay placeholder—not live Salomon excerpts.) In a live run, ask Salomon whether bounce copy is visible—not silent.

XO MCP — (Replay placeholder—condensed from stub XO text.) Same catalog snapshot as the row above (one epic-wide search in this replay). For HRREC-92003, locate bounce/complaint surfaces in metadata or REST contracts—directory names alone do not prove telemetry. Risk — Same catalog-level risk as rows above—confirm wiring for this key.
  • Finding: For “Recruiter sees error messages if their em...”, the three lenses mostly line up; biggest residual note: minor residual signals—confirm in a live evidence pass.
  • Recommended next step: Tighten Jira with two recruiter-visible checks for “Recruiter sees error messages if their email ...”.

Beyond Jira — Generic / Default Not-Delivered (Hard Bounce)
Given A recruiter with Modify/Send on conversational email where this slice applies on a tenant staged for this epic; Jira’s first *Given* already includes: an outbound email fails to deliver and the pro...
When A hard bounce or complaint path fires for a recruiting email in this slice; I open the thread or reporting surfaces Jira names
Then I see the specific failure headline/body or task status Jira promises — not a silent send without surfaced handling

Delivery failure surfaces
Given the integration produces a hard bounce or complaint path the story references
When I review the thread and any notification or reporting surfaces in scope
Then I see the specific status or copy promised (not a silent drop without analytics or suppression alignment)

Beyond Jira — Candidate email change hides actions and displays an info message

Privacy purge or retention edge

When I reopen the thread after purge completes
Then I see the documented empty or no-retained-messages state and purged content does not reappear via notifications or caches
